segments.text: This brand new telescope is working with
James Webb, but showing us something
totally new. It has a way larger field
of view than Web, thanks to its 18
but it's got a much smaller mirror,
meaning that it won't collect as much
light and be as detailed as Web's
images. And that's why the two will work
together with web seeing finer details
deeper into the universe and farther
back in time and Roman seeing much more
of the sky surveying thousands of square
degrees catching events that we've never
seen before and telling web where to
study more closely. Rome's going to
launch in 2027. So to learn more
